chapter_context.summary: Praises quiet peace over strife, highlights the crown of grandchildren, rebukes briberies, commends a merry heart as medicine, and advises guarding the lips.

outline.text: A Dry Morsel, the Refining Pot, and Crown of Old Men
outline.explanation: This section focuses on A Dry Morsel, the Refining Pot, and Crown of Old Men (17:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Excellent Speech, Deceitful Rewards, and Halting Strife Early
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Excellent Speech, Deceitful Rewards, and Halting Strife Early (17:7-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Justifying Wicked, Fool's Heart, and Merry Heart Medicine
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Justifying Wicked, Fool's Heart, and Merry Heart Medicine (17:15-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Bribery, Wisdom in Face of Prudent, and Guarding Lips
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Bribery, Wisdom in Face of Prudent, and Guarding Lips (17:23-28), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

key_themes.title: Wisdom vs. Folly
key_themes.description: Proverbs outlines a practical blueprint for daily living, contrasting the actions, words, and consequences of the wise with those of the foolish.

key_themes.title: The Fear of the Lord
key_themes.description: The foundational principle of wisdom is a reverent awe and trust in God, which guides moral choices and shapes character.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the practical sayings of Chapter 17, what is one specific area of your speech, relationships, or work where you can apply divine wisdom today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose the path of integrity and diligence over convenience or compromise?

cultural_historical.title: Ancient Near Eastern Wisdom Literature
cultural_historical.text: Proverbs shares structural and thematic similarities with other ancient wisdom writings, but stands unique in placing the Fear of Yahweh as the foundation of all true wisdom.
